content.description: Connecting a new cluster to AMD Resource Manager is currently an alpha-stage feature. Please contact AMD support for detailed information on how to proceed. You will be provided secrets in subsequent steps that will be used to securely connect your cluster to AMD Resource Manager.
Once you have received detailed instructions from AMD Support, please use these secrets to connect your cluster to AMD Resource Manager.
content.note: Note that these secrets will only be displayed once for security!
content.confirmation: I have copied the secrets to connect my cluster to AMD Resource Manager.

content.description: After the installation has been completed successfully, the cluster will attempt to establish a connection with the AMD Resource Manager. You should see the cluster become active in the list of connected clusters. The process might take some time to complete.

notifications.failure: Failed to request to connect a new cluster. Please try again later.

workloadsBaseUrl.label: Base URL for workloads submitted to the cluster.
workloadsBaseUrl.placeholder: Provide base URL for the workloads
workloadsBaseUrl.emptyError: Workloads base URL must not be empty

error.invalid: Workloads base URL must be a valid URL (must start with http:// or https://)

kubeApiUrl.label: Kube API URL for the cluster.
kubeApiUrl.emptyError: Kube API URL must not be empty

error.invalid: Kube API URL must be a valid URL (must start with http:// or https://)

config.title: View cluster kubeconfig
config.description: You can use the following kubeconfig to connect to the cluster {{name}} via kubectl or similar tools.
config.disabled: Kube API URL has not yet been configured for the cluster.

confirmation.title: Confirm Deletion
confirmation.description: Are you sure you want to delete this cluster? This action is irreversible.

notification.error: Failed to delete cluster. Please try again.

confirmation.title: Confirm Cancellation
confirmation.description: Are you sure you want to cancel this cluster connection request?

notification.error: Failed to cancel cluster request. Please try again.

gpuType.title: GPU Type
gpuType.tooltip: The GPU model or type available on this node.

gpuMemory.title: GPU Memory
gpuMemory.tooltip: Total GPU memory across all devices on this node.

cpuCores.title: CPU Cores
cpuCores.tooltip: The number of CPU cores available on this node.

systemMemory.title: System Memory
systemMemory.tooltip: The total system memory (RAM) on this node.

deviceMetrics.title: Device metrics
deviceMetrics.gpuCoreUtilizationTooltip: Percentage of GPU core (gfx) activity over time. Indicates how busy the GPU compute engines are.
deviceMetrics.noData: No GPU utilization data for the selected time range.
deviceMetrics.noTemperatureData: No GPU temperature data for the selected time range.
deviceMetrics.noPowerData: No GPU power consumption data for the selected time range.

nodes.title: Available nodes
nodes.tooltip: Total number of nodes available in the cluster. AMD Resource Manager routinely pulls node information from the cluster including resources and health.

projects.title: Assigned projects
projects.tooltip: Projects assigned to the cluster resources. Projects can be assigned guaranteed resources on cluster, or can assiged no resources and run on best-effort basis.

gpus.title: Allocated GPUs
gpus.tooltip: Total number of GPUs allocated to quotas in the cluster. Users belonging to projects with GPU quotas are guaranteed allocated GPU resources for their workloads.

workloads.title: Running workloads
workloads.tooltip: Total number of active workloads running on the cluster. Workloads submitted via AMD Resource Manager optimally scheduled, and tracked and monitored for resource usage and health.

clusters.title: Clusters
clusters.tooltip: Clusters for your organization that have been connected to AMD Resource Manager. Connecting clusters allows you to manage resources, quotas, and workloads on them.

nodes.title: Available nodes
nodes.tooltip: Total number of nodes available across all clusters. AMD Resource Manager routinely pulls node information from clusters including resources and health.

gpus.title: Allocated GPUs
gpus.tooltip: Total number of GPUs allocated to quotas, across all clusters. Users belonging to projects with GPU quotas are guaranteed allocated GPU resources for their workloads, in the corresponding clusters.

workloads.title: Running workloads
workloads.tooltip: Total number of active workloads across all clusters. Workloads submitted via AMD Resource Manager optimally scheduled, and tracked and monitored for resource usage and health.
